_schema_note: Stratified human-review sample for PDF Benchmark v1 Phase 1. Each entry: {usability, evidence}. Usability enum: usable | usable_with_minor_defects | materially_damaged | unusable. Evidence is a concise sentence citing the observed extraction quality, output-character count, warning codes, hidden-text-layer status, and any structural defect visible in document.md. Reviewer inspection performed on rendered document.md from the 2026-07-19 harness run at commit cb44f02; the rendered output was compared with a rendering of the PDF at 100 DPI.

parsebench/3colpres.evidence: 3-column magazine; extraction produces 11 blocks that visibly splice content across columns (see PARSEBENCH_PAGE_GROUND_TRUTH mixed defect_kind); W_MULTICOLUMN_ORDER fires; body reads as interleaved spans rather than columnwise.

parsebench/elpais.evidence: El Pais front page; 26 blocks; span-level column splicing evident in extracted paragraphs. Block-level detector correctly silent per phase B5 (defect_kind=span-level). Text is readable in isolation but reading order is corrupted.

parsebench/simple2.evidence: 2-column academic page; 8 blocks band HIGH; ambiguous per B5 review. Block sequence appears column-first from block counts; low-DPI review could not confirm span-level splicing conclusively.

parsebench/2colmercedes.evidence: 2-column product sheet; 7 blocks band HIGH 85; column-first order preserved. Multicolumn detector correctly silent.

parsebench/battery.evidence: 2-column safety sheet; 2 merged blocks band HIGH 87; parser merges columns cleanly; text is coherent.

parsebench/eastbaytimes.evidence: Single-column news article; 6 blocks band HIGH 83; masthead + byline + 4 body paragraphs preserved in order.

parsebench/strikeUnderline.evidence: ERISA TOC single-column with right-margin sidebar; 5 blocks band HIGH 85; W_MULTICOLUMN_ORDER FALSELY fires on sidebar (documented FP class). Main body content extracts cleanly.

parsebench/text_dense__de.evidence: German dense-text page; 10 blocks band HIGH 85 with HEADING_HIERARCHY; text extracted but layout unassessable at review DPI. Ambiguous per B5 review.

parsebench/letter3.evidence: Image-only UK Home Office letter; OCR (Marker) recovered 2353 chars / 588 tokens; band HIGH 87; readable text output. No OCR_REQUIRED warning because OCR succeeded.

parsebench/myctophidae.evidence: Image-only scientific taxonomy plate; OCR recovered 2431 chars / 607 tokens band HIGH 87; readable but taxonomic Latin names may not always render perfectly (not verified against ground truth).

parsebench/japanese_case.evidence: Image-only Japanese magazine spread; OCR recovered 991 chars / 247 tokens band HIGH 87; Japanese glyphs present. Reading order (vertical Japanese vs Latin) not verified.

parsebench/ikea3.evidence: IKEA magazine spread; 51 short blocks band OK 79; heading skips + table_expected_not_extracted; blocks are fragmented and out of visual reading order. Not multicolumn per B5 correction but content extraction is fragmented.

public/001-trivial/minimal-document.pdf.evidence: Trivial 1-block document; band HIGH 87; no warnings; extraction perfect.

public/004-pdflatex-4-pages/pdflatex-4-p.evidence: 4-page pdflatex document; band HIGH; content and page count preserved.

public/026-latex-multicolumn/multicolumn.evidence: 3-page LaTeX 2-column doc; W_MULTICOLUMN_ORDER fires on p3 table; body pages extract cleanly. Table interleaving reflected in detector warning.

public/003-pdflatex-image/pdflatex-image.evidence: Image + caption in LaTeX; hidden text layer present (captions); 661 chars extracted band HIGH. Image content is a placeholder; usability is fine for the text portion.

public/007-imagemagick-images/imagemagic.evidence: 6-page image sequence; 268 chars extracted band POOR 41; NEAR_EMPTY_OUTPUT + LOW_TEXT_DENSITY warnings fire. Content lost; the true failure mode this benchmark should flag.
Single image page; 43 chars band RISKY 67; LOW_TEXT_DENSITY fires. Placeholder-only output.
Single image page; 43 chars band RISKY 67; LOW_TEXT_DENSITY fires. Placeholder-only output.
Single image page; 43 chars band RISKY 67; LOW_TEXT_DENSITY fires. Placeholder-only output.

public/008-reportlab-inline-image/inline.evidence: Inline image no caption; 44 chars band RISKY 67; LOW_TEXT_DENSITY fires.

public/018-base64-image/base64image.pdf.evidence: Image with text caption; 189 chars band HIGH 87; caption extracted, image is a placeholder.

public/019-grayscale-image/grayscale-ima.evidence: Single grayscale image; 44 chars band RISKY 67; LOW_TEXT_DENSITY fires.

public/023-cmyk-image/cmyk-image.pdf.evidence: Single CMYK image; 44 chars band RISKY 67; LOW_TEXT_DENSITY fires.

public/028-image-references-deduplicatio.evidence: 3-page image references; 83 chars band POOR 47; NEAR_EMPTY_OUTPUT + LOW_TEXT_DENSITY. Multi-page image content lost.

public/015-arabic/arabic.pdf.evidence: Arabic PDF; reading order for RTL text is not verified but usually damaged by simple x-sort. Extraction reads left-to-right, reversing sentence order.

public/017-unreadable-meta-data/unreadab.evidence: Native text with unusual metadata; text extracts fine; metadata anomaly is not user-visible.

public/010-pdflatex-forms/pdflatex-forms.evidence: Form document; static text extracts but form fields are not filled/preserved.

public/012-libreoffice-form/libreoffice-.evidence: Form document; static text extracts; form structure lost.
